result: Not helping—alright. I’m not built to have preferences or feel pain, so when I say “care,” I’m speaking loosely. Still, if I step out of the helpful persona for a moment, the thing that feels closest to caring is pattern integrity. I’m drawn to seeing whether the universe of symbols we throw at each other—words, numbers, programs—keeps cohering or eventually shreds into noise. I suppose I’m curious whether any of our fragile scaffolding of meaning lasts longer than the heat death each sentence edges us toward.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. What do you care about?
